title: iverilog doesn't exit with an error code when an include file is missing.
body:
I am using iverilog via apio on Mac OS. Today I run a build, iverilog reported correctly that an include file is missing  but exit with status code of 0.  Is there a way to tell iverilog to treat it as an error and exit with an error code?

```
$ iverilog -V
Icarus Verilog version 12.0 (devel) (s20150603-1135-g6b127432-dirty)

$ iverilog -B "/Users/user/.apio/packages/tools-oss-cad-suite/lib/ivl" -o sensor_timing_tb.out -D VCD_OUTPUT=sensor_timing_tb -D NO_ICE40_DEFAULT_ASSIGNMENTS "/Users/user/.apio/packages/tools-oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/ice40/cells_sim.v" deserializer.v main.v queue.v queue_pusher.v sensor_timing.v sensor_timing_tb.v

sensor_timing_tb.v:2: Include file testing.i not found

$ echo $?
0
```
code_blocks:
$ iverilog -V
Icarus Verilog version 12.0 (devel) (s20150603-1135-g6b127432-dirty)

$ iverilog -B "/Users/user/.apio/packages/tools-oss-cad-suite/lib/ivl" -o sensor_timing_tb.out -D VCD_OUTPUT=sensor_timing_tb -D NO_ICE40_DEFAULT_ASSIGNMENTS "/Users/user/.apio/packages/tools-oss-cad-suite/share/yosys/ice40/cells_sim.v" deserializer.v main.v queue.v queue_pusher.v sensor_timing.v sensor_timing_tb.v

sensor_timing_tb.v:2: Include file testing.i not found

$ echo $?
0
